note: Login Destination exposes a login_destination config entity ('rule') with trigger + role/page/language conditions + destination, ordered by weight, plus a small settings config object. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded), probing documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site, which configuration entity type does the Login Destination module use to store a redirect rule, and where in the admin UI do I create one?
evals.expected_output: The `login_destination` config entity (a 'rule'). Create/manage rules at Admin > Configuration > People > Login destinations (/admin/config/people/login-destination), route login_destination.list.

evals.prompt: What are the four trigger events a Login Destination rule can redirect on?
evals.expected_output: Registration, Login, One-time login link, and Logout (machine names registration, login, one-time-login, logout).

evals.prompt: I want a Login Destination rule that only redirects users who logged in with the 'editor' role from the /user login page. Which rule conditions do I set, and what happens if I leave roles empty?
evals.expected_output: Set the roles condition to 'editor' and the pages condition to the /user path (pages_type: only the listed pages). If no roles are selected the rule applies to all users; only one selected role needs to match.

evals.prompt: If two enabled Login Destination rules both match a login, which one decides the redirect and how do I control that?
evals.expected_output: Rules are sorted by weight and the first matching enabled rule wins; order rules by weight to set priority.

evals.prompt: Can I use a token like [user:name] in a Login Destination rule's destination path, and what values besides a plain internal path are accepted?
evals.expected_output: Yes — user and global tokens are replaced at redirect time. The destination can also be a node (autocomplete), an external URL, <front>, or <current> (the page the user came from).

evals.prompt: I want Login Destination to redirect a user right after they click a one-time login link, before they reach the password-change form. Which setting and config object controls this?
evals.expected_output: Enable immediate_redirect (default false) on the login_destination.settings config object ('Redirect immediately after using one-time login link').

evals.prompt: Which Login Destination setting makes Drupal's own ?destination= query parameter take priority over the module's own rules?
evals.expected_output: The preserve_destination key (default false) on login_destination.settings ('Preserve the destination parameter'); note the login block redirect stops working when it is enabled.

evals.prompt: What permission gates managing Login Destination rules and settings?
evals.expected_output: 'administer login destination settings' — the single permission gating all login destination admin routes and the config entity's admin_permission.
